headline: U.S. Strikes Iran After Ceasefire Violation
detail: Active U.S. military action in the Middle East triggers immediate risk-off sentiment, pushing investors out of high-beta tech and growth names. Your entire book — AVGO, PLTR, CRWD, QQQ, SMH — is exactly the kind of exposure that gets sold in these moments. On margin, that rotation hits your account harder than the index numbers suggest.

headline: SpaceX Bonds Flash Warning, AI Financing Concerns Rise
detail: Investors are pumping the brakes on SpaceX credit, which directly pressures the narrative around SPCX and SPAL, your two SpaceX-linked positions. SPAL is already down nearly 17% and uses 2x leverage, meaning any further SpaceX weakness hits you twice. This headline is a specific, same-day risk to those two names.

headline: Oracle Worst Week Since 2001 Dot-Com Bust on AI Financing Fears
detail: Oracle's collapse signals the market is seriously questioning whether AI infrastructure spending can be sustained — that's a direct headwind for chip and AI-adjacent names like AVGO and SMH. This isn't isolated noise; it's a sector-wide repricing of AI growth assumptions that sits at the core of your portfolio.

headline: Micron Sinks 6% in Wild Week of Trading
detail: Micron's sharp drop adds to the pressure on the broader semiconductor trade, which hits SMH and reinforces the SELL signal on AVGO. Your SMH position still shows a 10% gain and is on HOLD, but a deteriorating chip tape could threaten that cushion — worth watching your $540 stop there.
